blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This one is a real hidden gem on Allgood Road. It's a Mexican grocery store with a taqueria in the back, and the food coming out of that kitchen is genuinely excellent. Don't let the setup fool you. The people who know about it keep coming back.

The al pastor is the move here. It's well seasoned, not too crispy, and cooked exactly right. The bean and cheese pupusas are 10 out of 10, and the chicken plate with rice and beans is worth ordering too. The beans are made from scratch, not from a can, and you can tell. The tortas are great as well if you want something handheld.

This is a cash-and-carry kind of spot, cheap and fast. Perfect for lunch or a quick weeknight dinner when you want real food without a big bill.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Laredos is our go-to on Sandy Plains when we want solid Mexican food without a lot of fuss. The inside is colorful and festive, and it has that cantina feel that makes it work for a family dinner or a casual night out with friends. The margaritas are a big draw and worth ordering.

They do all the Mexican classics well. It's the kind of place where everyone at the table finds something they want, and the portions are generous for the price. Good for a weeknight when you don't want to cook but also don't want to spend a lot.

They're open every day until at least 10pm, which is handy when the dinner decision happens late.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA

blurb:
Los Bravos is the kind of Mexican place the neighborhood keeps coming back to. It's casual, open every day, and the food from the kitchen is consistently good. Good for a family dinner, a quick lunch, or just when you want solid Mexican food without a long drive.

The food is the reason people go. Long-time regulars will tell you the kitchen delivers. Just go in knowing the margaritas have gotten pricey compared to other spots in the area, so if you're watching the bar tab, that's worth keeping in mind.

They're open late most nights, which is handy if dinner runs later than planned.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is a genuine find. The owner is warm and will actually talk you through the menu, which is great if you're new to Vietnamese food. They take real pride in how they make their pho, and you can taste the difference. It's a 4.8 with over 500 reviews, which is rare for a place this low-key.

The pho is the thing to get here. Reviewers who tried it for the first time were blown away. It's the kind of bowl that makes you understand why people are so loyal to good pho.

Prices are very reasonable, it's open seven days a week until 9pm, and it works well for a quick lunch or an easy weeknight dinner.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the place you go when you want to make a night of it. It sits right on the Chattahoochee off Powers Ferry, and the setting alone is worth the trip. We've taken out-of-town guests here more than once and it always lands well. Good for a birthday dinner, anniversary, or anytime you want something that feels like a real occasion.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, and the waterfront views make everything feel a little more special. It's not a cheap night out, so go in with that expectation, but most people feel like it's worth it.

Sunday hours are split with a break between 4pm and 5pm, so plan accordingly if you're thinking about Sunday dinner.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
J. Christopher's is the kind of place you end up at on a Saturday morning when you want a real breakfast without any fuss. It's a daytime-only spot, open every day until 2pm, so it works great for a lazy weekend brunch or a quick lunch during the week. The vibe is relaxed and friendly, nothing fancy.

They do classic breakfast and lunch fare well. Omelets are a go-to here, and they rotate seasonal specials that are worth checking out. If you're more in a lunch mood, the burgers are solid too.

Just keep in mind they close at 2pm every day, so this is not a dinner option. Get there before the weekend brunch crowd fills it up.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Heirloom Market is a bit of a hidden gem over on Akers Mill, and if you haven't been, it's worth making the trip. It's tiny, there's basically one communal table inside, and the whole setup is no-frills. But the BBQ has a Korean-influenced twist that makes it stand out from every other barbecue place in the area. Good for a casual lunch or an early dinner when you just want great food without a lot of fuss.

People come back for the smoked meats, and the Korean flavors mixed into the barbecue are what make it different from the usual spots. It's the kind of place that has a real following for good reason.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday and close at 8pm, and they're closed Sunday and Monday. It gets busy, so going a little early is a smart move.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Sunnyside is the pizza place I keep sending people to when they ask for a recommendation in Sandy Springs. It has a 4.7 with over a thousand reviews, which pretty much says it all. Good for a casual family dinner or a night out when you want something reliable and really good.

The Hot Honey Charred pizza is the one everyone talks about, and the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are worth ordering as a starter. People rave about the sauce on those wings. The Caesar salad is a simple, well-executed classic if you want something on the side.

They're closed on Mondays, so plan around that.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is the kind of place you go when you want Chinese food that goes beyond the usual takeout menu. They do hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce, so there's more going on here than your average neighborhood spot. Good for a group dinner when everyone wants something a little different.

The lobster in black bean sauce is the standout dish worth trying if you're feeling adventurous, and the BBQ duck is a solid order for anyone who likes roasted meats done right.

They're open until 10pm every night, which makes it a decent option when you want a late dinner without much planning.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you want a reliable slice close to home, JJ's on Roswell Road is the place. It's the kind of neighborhood pizza spot you end up going back to over and over because it just never lets you down. Good for a weeknight dinner, picking up on the way home, or feeding the family without a lot of fuss.

The stuffed pizza is the move if you haven't tried it yet. One slice is genuinely a full meal, and people rave about it. The regular pies are solid too, and the prices are fair for what you get.

Just note they're closed on Tuesdays, so plan around that.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is a fun one if you haven't been. It's a Brazilian açaí shop, and the whole concept is self-serve, so you load up your bowl with exactly what you want. The staff is genuinely friendly and the vibe is casual and laid back. Great for an afternoon treat or a post-workout stop with the kids.

The açaí bowls are the main draw, but they also have crepes and coxinha, which is a Brazilian chicken croquette worth trying if you've never had one. It's a nice little taste of something different without going far from home.

They're closed on Tuesdays, but open until 10pm the rest of the week, which is handy.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Royal Spice is the spot to know in Sandy Springs if you're into Indian food. The staff is genuinely helpful and patient if you have questions about the menu, which matters when you're ordering something unfamiliar. The dining room is quiet and comfortable, a good choice for a weeknight dinner or a low-key date night.

People come back for the entrees and say the appetizers are worth ordering too. The servers will help you figure out what to get based on what you like, so don't be shy about asking.

Just note there's a break in service on weekdays between 2:30pm and 4:30pm, so it's not a great fit for a mid-afternoon meal.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
